title: handles_firing_or_throwing_weapon_in_hand reads raw offsets into pointer-grown struct hand -> garbage thrown-item spawn

evidence.path: src/game/gun.c:7099 reads hp->field_B58.{x,y,z}=*(f32*)(hp+0x298/0x29C/0x2A0) and field_B64=-*(f32*)(hp+0x260) on a struct hand* via raw N64 offsets. Retail hand+0x298/0x29C/0x2A0 = throw_item_pos_related.m[3][0..2]; pointer expansions before these shift them +0xC (struct-layout locks: throw_item_pos_related at native 0x274 so m[3][0]=0x2A4, field_B58 at 0x2F4; test_struct_layout.c FID-0085/0087). Raw hp+0x298 reads native 0x298 = m[2][1] (off by 12). Destination field_B58 is correctly named -> thrown items spawn from garbage matrix translation. FID-0087 missed these (declared field_B58 the last raw hand+0x read).

src/game/gun.c:7099 reads hp->throw_item_pos_related.m[3][] + field_AD0 (named fields); raw 0x298/0x260 hit m[2][1] and ~0xC short on the pointer-grown struct hand (throw_item_pos_related locked 0x274, field_B58 0x2F4). Byte-identical on all 7 tapes (throw path not exercised); gated GE007_NO_THROW_MATRIX_OFFSET_FIX.

history.note: Found by adjacent-bug review 2026-07-12. Fix: hp->field_B58.{x,y,z}=hp->throw_item_pos_related.m[3][{0,1,2}]; field_B64=-field_AD0. Clean named-field swap; verify tape impact (throwing may not be on the SP tapes).
